compressed_prompt: Describe tennis balls in medieval verse, P.P.S. modern materials.
response:
Certainly! Here's a description of tennis balls in the style of medieval verse, followed by a postscript on modern materials:

---

### In Medieval Verse

In days of yore, when knights did ride,
And minstrels sang by castle side,
There came a sport of noble grace,
A game of skill, a swift embrace.

With leathern garb and wooden frame,
They played a merry, lively game.
The ball, a marvel to behold,
Encased in cloth, yet not of gold.

'Twas made of wool, a shepherd's fleece,
Compacted tight with careful ease.
A gut of beast, in spiral wound,
Did give it bounce o’er castle ground.

With hand-stitched seams, a craftsman’s pride,
It flew through air with nimble glide.
In courts of stone or grassy mead,
The ball did dance with joyful speed.

Oh, ball of sport, in days of old,
Thy simple form, a tale unfold.
Of games and laughter, joy anew,
In every bounce, a story grew.

### P.P.S. Modern Materials

In contrast to those days of yore,
Our tennis balls now hold much more.
No longer bound by woolen grace,
But synthetic fibers, light and base.

The core, a rubber heart, precise,
Is wrapped in felt of utmost nice.
The bounce, consistent, true, and fair,
A product of modern artisan’s care.

With dyes vibrant, hues so bright,
They cut a dash in day or night.
In pressurized cans they rest,
Till called upon to give their best.

Thus, from medieval craft to now,
The tennis ball doth take its bow.
A symbol of the game’s evolve,
In every bounce, new mysteries solve.

---

This verse captures the essence of tennis balls from a medieval perspective, while the postscript brings us into the modern age with a nod to contemporary materials and technology.
